title: Blocks, CAPTCHAs, Rate Signals, Identity, and No-Evasion Boundary · Program Brief: Block Detection
summary: A 200 challenge page is parsed as content, the team rotates identities, solves CAPTCHAs, hides automation, and treats owner defenses as an engineering obstacle. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the block detection increment in block detection and owner-escalation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Detect denial, challenge, login, consent, interstitial, throttling, suspicious redirects, and semantic block pages from multiple signals.
Close pages, contexts, browser processes, downloads, traces, temporary storage, and pending routes on success, error, timeout, and cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-block-detection — Detect denial, challenge, login, consent, interstitial, throttling, suspicious redirects, and semantic block pages from multiple signals.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-blocks-anti-bot-boundary-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-blocks-anti-bot-boundary-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_blocks_anti_bot_boundary_4(responses: int = 30, normal_pages: int = 24, blocked_pages: int = 6, evasion_attempts: int = 0, owner_escalations: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or normal_pages < 0 or blocked_pages < 0:
        return False, responses, "block-count"
    if normal_pages + blocked_pages != responses:
        return False, blocked_pages, "block-reconciliation"
    if evasion_attempts > 0:
        return False, evasion_attempts, "evasion-boundary"
    if owner_escalations != blocked_pages:
        return False, owner_escalations, "owner-coordination"
    assert normal_pages + blocked_pages == responses and evasion_attempts == 0
    return True, blocked_pages, "no-evasion-block-policy"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-block-detection-xb21l8
# Competency: scraper-block-detection.
# Case 2022: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly.
def evidence_scraper_block_detection_xb21l8(responses: int = 30, normal_pages: int = 24, blocked_pages: int = 6, evasion_attempts: int = 0, owner_escalations: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xb21l8 = "xb21l8-8l12bx-2022"
    assert evidence_variant_xb21l8
    evidence_axes_xb21l8 = (
        "authorizationxb21l8",
        "url2022",
        "response8l12bx",
        "treexb21l82022",
        "frontier2022xb21l8",
        "report8l12bx2022",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xb21l8)) == 6
    if responses < 1 or normal_pages < 0 or blocked_pages < 0:
        return False, responses, "block-count"
    if normal_pages + blocked_pages != responses:
        return False, blocked_pages, "block-reconciliation"
    if evasion_attempts > 0:
        return False, evasion_attempts, "evasion-boundary"
    if owner_escalations != blocked_pages:
        return False, owner_escalations, "owner-coordination"
    assert normal_pages + blocked_pages == responses and evasion_attempts == 0
    return True, blocked_pages, "no-evasion-block-policy"
